title: Nanodrop Spectrophotometer (ND-1000) for Nucleic Acid Measurement
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.id2ca8e
materials: Blank sample (buffer or RNase free H2O)

steps.instruction: Open ND-1000 V3.8.1 program using the computer connected to the Nanodrop
Once the program is opened, click on the corresponding application module (e.g., Nucleic acid to determine the concentration and purity of nucleic acid)
Open the sampling arm and load a blank sample (e.g., buffer or RNase free H 2 O) undetermined
Close the arm on the machine to cover the blank sample
When the machine is finished, open the sampling arm and clean the blank off the the upper and lower pedestals with a Kimwipe
Load your DNA or RNA sample and close the arm undetermined
Click on the sample type in the upper right-hand corner of the program (e.g., DNA or RNA)
Click "Measure" in the upper left hand corner of the program
Once the machine is finished reading the sample, read the 260/280, 260/230 and concentration (ng/µL) measurements.
Clean the nucleic acid sample off of the upper and lower pedestals with a Kimwipe
